acceptanceCriteria.description: curl -i https://browzeremb.com/api/telemetry/usage with a valid bearer returns 202 (or method-not-allowed for GET); 404 is treated as a regression.

After deleting the only workspace in an org via DELETE /api/workspaces/:id, GET /api/billing/usage returns workspaces_count=0, chunks_count=0, storage_bytes=0 within 30 seconds (no manual UPDATE needed).
DELETE /api/workspaces/:id with a default-role member API key returns 204 (was 403); the audit log records the delete.
On a fresh `browzer sync` with a default member key the stderr stream contains zero occurrences of 'could not fetch billing usage'.
browzer explore '<seeded query>' --json returns every entry with score in [0.0, 1.0] AND rawScore present.
browzer ask '<seeded query>' --json returns sources with score in [0.0, 1.0] AND rawScore present; identical-score collapse across all rows is a regression.
apps/rag/CLAUDE.md line ~12 no longer claims universal preSerialization coverage; the amended text references both the apps/rag plugin and the apps/api shared helper.
scripts/audit/score-coverage-smoke.sh exists, exits 0 on a clean run, exits non-zero when any of the four surfaces returns a score outside [0, 1] or missing rawScore on explore/ask.
apps/rag log output for /search, /explore, /deps, /ask responses includes a JSON line with the keys surface, inputCount, inputMin, inputMax, outputMin, outputMax.

On a zero-workspace org, click UPLOAD NEW on /dashboard/documents → either Create Workspace modal opens (preferred) or button is explicitly disabled with tooltip; the DOM is observably different post-click.
Direct navigation to /dashboard/settings/billing renders billing-specific content (h1 != 'Configurações', or h1 'Configurações > Plano & Cobrança'); same for /settings/org and /settings/api-tokens.
/dashboard/documents on a zero-workspace org renders a 'Create workspace' affordance that on submit POSTs /api/workspaces and refreshes the list.
Sidebar nav contains an item that links to /dashboard/members (visible to authenticated users with the Members capability).
Post-key-creation snapshot on /dashboard/api-keys contains exactly one button matching name 'Issue Key' (no duplicate empty-state CTA).
/dashboard/search and /dashboard/ask with no workspace selected display helper text (or visible affordance) explaining the precondition next to the submit button.
After a successful file upload, a toast appears within 2s AND the AWAITING INGESTION feed shows a row for the uploaded doc with non-zero counters.
After CLI sync indexes N source files + M docs, /dashboard/documents DOCUMENTS_INDEXED card reads the same value as the per-workspace card's FILES counter (no '0 vs 6' contradiction).
/dashboard/ask SOURCES card renders documentName + snippet + link per source row (no empty gray bars).
Every dashboard surface (sidebar + KB + Search + Ask + Settings + Members) renders in a single language for the authenticated user; mixed-language is a regression.
/dashboard/documents upload accepts a file via Playwright's locator('input[type=file]').setInputFiles(...) on the visible target (no need to address a hidden input).
/register shows a spinner OR a multi-step indicator during the 'Provisioning…' window (no static label for >2s).
SOURCE DOCUMENTS table on /dashboard/documents lists only docs returned by GET /api/workspaces/:id/documents; rogue CLAUDE.md rows are absent on a sandbox without CLAUDE.md.
/dashboard/ask answer text exceeds one sentence when 5 sources are present and the seeded prompt warrants a multi-sentence answer (regression test on the seeded sandbox).

After a manual DROP SCHEMA public CASCADE on the apps/auth Postgres, a fresh apps/auth boot completes its drizzle migration cycle without 500 on the first /api/auth/* request that needs the device_code table.

(closes F-19)

DELETE /api/workspaces/:id decrements org_usage_current.workspaces_count AND chunks_count AND storage_bytes for the affected organization, mirroring the create-side debit via outbox_usage_delta. (closes F-12, F-25)

API keys with the default 'member' role can delete and re-create workspaces they own; the RBAC matrix in apps/auth and the requireAuthz check in apps/api are updated together so DELETE /api/workspaces/:id stops returning 403 for the canonical member-role API key. (closes F-11)

browzer sync no longer prints '⚠ could not fetch billing usage: Forbidden — your token does not have access to this resource.' on every successful run; either grant API keys read scope on /api/billing/usage or downgrade the message to debug. (closes F-13)
GET /api/workspaces/:id/explore wraps results in normalizeScores so every entry's score is in [0, 1] and a rawScore field carries the un-normalized value. (closes F-20)

POST /ask sources are normalized before envelope serialization: every DedupedSource has score in [0, 1] AND a rawScore field, applied via the shared @browzer/core/search/score-normalize helper. (closes F-21)

apps/rag/CLAUDE.md line 12 doc claim is amended to describe the actual coverage: preSerialization in apps/rag covers /search; explore + deps + ask normalize via the shared helper in apps/api. (closes F-22)

scripts/audit/score-coverage-smoke.sh exists and asserts that every score field returned by explore, deps, search, and ask falls in [0, 1] against a seeded sandbox; runs as part of the CI quality job. (closes F-23)

apps/rag score-normalize plugin emits a structured log line with surface, inputCount, inputMin, inputMax, outputMin, outputMax on every normalized response so production traffic is auditable without re-running the CLI. (closes F-24)

/dashboard/documents UPLOAD NEW button is functional regardless of workspace count: zero-workspace state opens the Create Workspace modal (FR-12); >0-workspace state opens the existing dropzone. No silent no-op DOM. (closes F-1)
/dashboard/settings/{org,billing,api-tokens} render section-specific content; each sub-route's page.tsx no longer collapses to the parent Settings hub. (closes F-2)

/dashboard/documents (and the empty WORKSPACE SCOPE panes on /search and /ask) expose a Create Workspace UI that POSTs to /api/workspaces with name (required) + rootPath (optional) and refreshes the list. (closes F-3)
Sidebar navigation surfaces a Members link that lands on /dashboard/members. (closes F-4)
/dashboard/api-keys renders exactly one Issue Key affordance regardless of empty vs populated state; the post-creation snapshot has a single button[name='Issue Key']. (closes F-5)

/dashboard/search and /dashboard/ask submit affordances surface why they are inert when no workspace is selected (helper text near the submit button or arrow toward the empty WORKSPACE SCOPE card). No silently-disabled state. (closes F-6)

After a successful file upload on /dashboard/documents, the user sees a toast or inline status row with doc id and queued/running/failed state; the AWAITING INGESTION feed updates in real time. (closes F-9)

/dashboard/documents DOCUMENTS_INDEXED card reads from the same source as CHUNKS_TOTAL / per-workspace FILES so the three counters are consistent post-sync (no '0 / 6 / 4' contradiction). (closes F-14)
/dashboard/ask SOURCES card renders document name, snippet, and link per source row instead of empty 'score: 0.010 ws:' placeholders. (closes F-16)
/dashboard/ask SOURCES scoring matches the underlying explore/search backend: identical-score collapse to 0.010 across all rows is treated as a regression. (closes F-17, depends on FR-6)

i18n is consistent across the dashboard: every authenticated surface (sidebar, KB, Search, Ask, Settings, Members) renders in the same language. Default scope: port /settings and /members copy to English to match the rest. (closes F-7)
/dashboard/documents upload affordance is an <input type='file'> styled as a drop target; programmatic setInputFiles works against the visible target. (closes F-8)
/register shows a 3-step progress indicator (Creating account → Provisioning organisation → Sending verification email) or at minimum a spinner during the ~6-8s 'Provisioning…' window. (closes F-10)
SOURCE DOCUMENTS table on /dashboard/documents lists only documents that physically exist in the workspace; the rogue CLAUDE.md row from a sandbox without CLAUDE.md no longer appears. (closes F-15)
/dashboard/ask answer text uses the model's full context for the configured plan; single-sentence truncation when 5 rich sources are present is treated as a regression. (closes F-18)

apps/auth/scripts/migrate.ts partial-state heuristic also fires when tableCount === 0 AND trackedCount > 0 (graph wiped, drizzle tracking schema survived); fresh boots after a manual DROP SCHEMA public CASCADE no longer 500 on the first INSERT. (closes F-28)

objectives: Close every P0 defect so click-only signup → workspace → upload → search/ask completes end-to-end on a fresh org without dropping to curl.
Restore parity between the dashboard surface and the underlying data layer for scores, source rendering, and document counters.
Make quota counters durable across delete/recreate cycles so org_usage_current matches graph reality after every workspace lifecycle event.
Ship the gateway telemetry proxy rule so production CLI daemon flushes are persisted and TOKENS_ECONOMIZED reflects actual savings.
Land an audit-script regression that catches future score normalization regressions across all four CLI surfaces.

Bug B (browzer init idempotency check via ListWorkspaces) — already shipped on the same branch.
F-26 (Neo4j volume hardening) and F-27 (MinIO volume hardening) — already remediated via Railway volume add ops.
Server-side workspace dedup (the 'workspace per user vs per org' product decision deferred per Round 5).

scripts/audit/railway-volume-coverage.sh (Round 5 follow-up #2) — separate audit-script scope, tracked elsewhere.
prd.overview: A live dogfood smoke of the dashboard + CLI surfaces against the host-mode stack on 2026-04-29 surfaced 25 findings spanning gateway routing, RBAC, quota reconciliation on delete, score-normalization coverage, dashboard UX, CLI and migration tooling. The empirical state is recorded in docs/browzer/dogfood-2026-04-29.md with reproducers and root-cause hypotheses per finding. Bug A (delete batching), Bug B (init idempotency), F-26 (Neo4j volume), F-27 (MinIO volume) were already remediated on the current branch fix-20260429-delete-batching-and-init-idempotent or via Railway ops and are out of scope. This PRD covers F-1..F-25 + F-28 so a fresh signup → click-only flow can provision a workspace, upload a doc, run search/ask and see correct counters / scores / sources without dropping to curl, and so production TOKENS_ECONOMIZED stops reading 0 forever.

personas.description: First-time dashboard user who only follows clicks after signup and expects to provision a workspace, upload, search, ask, and see counters update without leaving the product.
Self-hosted operator running browzer init / browzer sync on a real repo and consuming TOKENS_ECONOMIZED, scores, and source citations through the CLI + dashboard.
Org admin recovering from a delete-and-recreate cycle who must not be locked out by stale workspaces_count / chunks_count / storage_bytes counters.

risks.description: FR-3 (member RBAC) is a product decision, not a pure bug-fix; the alternate decision (admin-only delete) re-shapes FR-13 + the dashboard members flow.

risks.description: FR-2 chunks_delta + storage_delta enqueueing in the delete handler is async; AC-2's 30s window relies on the outbox-consumer cadence in worker-ops.
risks.mitigation: Validate the cadence under load before claiming AC-2; add a fast-path direct decrement if the lag exceeds 30s in practice.

risks.description: FR-19 (ask source scoring) depends on FR-6 (DedupedSource normalization); if FR-6 ships partial, the dashboard regression persists.
risks.mitigation: Order tasks: ship FR-6 before the dashboard side of FR-19; AC-6 covers both.

risks.description: F-15 rogue CLAUDE.md row may indicate that browzer sync walked outside the workspace root, which is broader than this PRD's scope.
risks.mitigation: FR-23 acceptance is 'matches /api/workspaces/:id/documents response'; if the API itself returns the bad row, escalate to a CLI-side investigation in update-docs.

findings.category: Missing upstream route
findings.description: apps/web/src/app/api/internal/workspaces/route.ts POSTs to /internal/workspaces?orgId=, but apps/api only registers GET. POST variant absent — FR-12/AC-12 broken in production.
findings.suggestedFix: Add app.post('/internal/workspaces', { preHandler: [serviceAuth] }) accepting body { name, rootPath } + ?orgId=, delegating to graphStore.createWorkspace + quotaGuardWorkspace; return 201.

findings.category: rawScore invariant not compile-time enforced
findings.description: Dedup loop seeds rawScore from src.score; max-merge compares src.score against existing.rawScore. Semantically same value today, but if normalizeScores ever runs upstream, comparison silently mixes normalised + un-normalised values.
findings.suggestedFix: Add code comment + runtime assertion confirming src.score is always un-normalised at this site; add unit test verifying rawScore equals max un-normalised value across multi-source dedup.

findings.category: Snapshot-failure vs zero-workspace fallback indistinguishable
findings.description: DELETE handler falls back to releaseWorkspace when snapshot fails (chunksSnapshot=0/bytesSnapshot=0n). Same path as a genuinely-empty workspace; operators can't distinguish in traces.
findings.suggestedFix: Add log field snapshotFailed: true|false in the info log at line 374 so production traces distinguish the cases.

findings.category: Member role gains org-wide delete vs ownership-scoped
findings.description: FR-3/AC-3 'member role can delete workspaces they own' — but seed grants resource_id: null (wildcard) so members can delete ANY workspace within their org. PRD R-2 acknowledges product decision; clarify intent.
findings.suggestedFix: Confirm with product owner: org-wide member delete (current) or ownership-scoped (then add createdByUserId check in DELETE handler for member role; admin/owner skip).

findings.category: CSRF semantics undocumented for server-side callers
findings.description: After F-1 fix, /api/internal/* routes through web → api. assertSameOrigin checks Origin against BETTER_AUTH_URL. Server-side fetch from Next.js components without Origin header would fail CSRF; current usage browser-only but undocumented.
findings.suggestedFix: Add code comment in route handler: server-actions/component fetch must include explicit Origin: <BETTER_AUTH_URL> if called server-side.

findings.category: @ts-nocheck suppresses type safety
findings.description: @ts-nocheck on the plugin file means TypeScript cannot catch normalizeScores signature regressions. Runtime logic correct; compile-time safety lost.
findings.suggestedFix: Remove @ts-nocheck; resolve individual cast sites with proper type guards. Constrain casts so signature changes surface as compile errors.

findings.category: releaseWorkspaceCounters non-transactional with Neo4j deletes
findings.description: DELETE handler runs Neo4j deletes THEN releaseWorkspaceCounters. Process crash between leaves counters stale until reconciliation-consumer runs. AC-2 30s SLA not met in crash-recovery path.
findings.suggestedFix: Document recovery path in handler comment: 'reconciliation-consumer corrects counter from graph state if Postgres UPDATE never runs; 30s SLA applies to happy path only.' Verify cadence in worker/CLAUDE.md.

findings.category: CSRF Origin header undocumented for non-browser callers
findings.description: Relative-URL fetch doesn't explicitly set Origin. Browser-originated same-origin fetch auto-includes Origin (OK). Risk for non-browser test runners (undici) that don't send Origin by default.
findings.suggestedFix: No production change. Integration tests calling route handler directly should set Origin: <BETTER_AUTH_URL> in fetch.

findings.category: score-coverage-smoke.sh always SKIP in quality job
findings.description: Smoke script exits 0 (SKIP) when BROWZER_SERVER/BROWZER_API_KEY unset. Quality job runs without those secrets — script always no-op. AC-8 regression assertion never fires in CI quality gate.
findings.suggestedFix: Move score-coverage-smoke.sh to e2e-smoke job (TEST_* secrets available) with fail-on-exit-2; OR add unit-mode path validating scoring logic against fixture JSON without live server.

findings.category: F-28 branch sentinel-table health check missing
findings.description: F-28 branch drops drizzle schema unconditionally on tableCount===0. Discriminator is fragile: partial table drops (not CASCADE) leave tableCount>0 but key tables missing. Low severity (ops-only).
findings.suggestedFix: Add comment explaining tableCount===0 reliability for DROP SCHEMA public CASCADE. Add post-migrate health check verifying sentinel table (device_code) exists before process exits 0.

findings.category: Semantic correctness — double normalization on /ask sources
findings.description: The comment claims src.score is the un-normalised pipeline score. Factually wrong: apps/rag's scoreNormalizePlugin (preSerialization) has already transformed src.score → (2/π)·atan(raw) and set src.rawScore = original BEFORE the response reaches apps/api. ask.ts ignores src.rawScore and feeds the already-normalized src.score back into normalizeScores(). The resulting /ask score is (2/π)·atan((2/π)·atan(rawPipeline)) — further compressed than /search. Contract test + score-coverage-smoke.sh both validate [0,1] which double-normalized still satisfies, so neither catches this drift.
findings.suggestedFix: Line 513: if ((src.rawScore ?? src.score) > existing.rawScore) existing.rawScore = src.rawScore ?? src.score; Lines 533-534: rawScore: src.rawScore ?? src.score. Score field at line 546 already reads from rawScore so it'll auto-correct.

findings.category: Missing quota guard on POST /internal/workspaces
findings.description: POST /internal/workspaces (service-secret route used by dashboard CreateWorkspaceModal) calls graphStore.createWorkspace() directly without quotaGuardWorkspace preHandler. The atomic debit (UPDATE org_usage_current SET workspaces_count + 1 WHERE count < limit) doesn't require a bearer principal — only orgId from query string. Free-plan org can create unlimited workspaces via dashboard while CLI path correctly enforces 402.
findings.suggestedFix: Build createWorkspaceQuotaGuardForOrg helper accepting orgId directly, or read plan limit + atomic debit inline before createWorkspace(). Match 402 envelope shape.

findings.category: CLAUDE.md default-ignore butterfly effect on existing workspaces
findings.description: DefaultIgnorePathSuffixes adds CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md. Next browzer sync for existing users with indexed CLAUDE.md (e.g. apps/web/CLAUDE.md, packages/cli/CLAUDE.md, root CLAUDE.md) will EMIT DELETE for those docs — silent removal from workspace graph. browzer explore + search lose CLAUDE.md content. Escape (.browzerignore !CLAUDE.md) documented in code but not in release notes. Node-side packages/shared/src/ignore-patterns.ts has no parallel file-level list.
findings.suggestedFix: (1) CHANGELOG release-note warning. (2) One-time migration opt-out flag on first sync after release. (3) Decide if Node ingestion mirrors file-level ignore (add DefaultIgnoreFileNames to shared). (4) Surface override path when a delete is suppressed by this rule.

findings.category: preWorkersClose hook lacks timeout — audit drain not guaranteed
findings.description: preWorkersClose .catch logs and continues — resilient. But if closeApiKeyAuditConsumer throws (Redis dropped, etc.), workers proceed to close() while in-flight audit Postgres inserts may still execute. registerGracefulShutdown's 30s SIGTERM timer is shared — if preWorkersClose blocks >30s on a large batch, process.exit fires before worker.close() finishes, recreating the orphan-job scenario the fix was meant to prevent.
findings.suggestedFix: Wrap in Promise.race([deps.preWorkersClose(), timeout(25_000)]) yielding 5s for worker.close(). WARN on timeout instead of fatal. Mirror BullMQ pattern: worker.pause() → drain → close with timeout.

findings.category: Synchronous releaseWorkspaceCounters: Neo4j+Postgres on request path
findings.description: DELETE handler runs listDocumentsInWorkspace (Neo4j ~2-5ms) sync before delete + releaseWorkspaceCounters (Postgres ~1-3ms) after. Both on HTTP path. No timeout on listDocumentsInWorkspace — stalled Neo4j read (lock contention from concurrent ingest) holds request indefinitely.
findings.suggestedFix: Promise.race(listDocumentsInWorkspace, timeout(3_000)); on timeout fall back to releaseWorkspace-only path + log warn. Caps worst-case latency from F-25 fix.

findings.category: GET /api/internal/workspaces lacks assertSameOrigin
findings.description: POST handler correctly calls assertSameOrigin. GET handler only requireOrg. While GET is read-only, returns org workspace list — credentialed cross-origin img/fetch could exfiltrate. Other /api/internal/* routes apply assertSameOrigin to all methods (per apps/web/CLAUDE.md). Inconsistency is the risk.
findings.suggestedFix: Add `const csrf = assertSameOrigin(request); if (csrf) return csrf;` at top of GET. Aligns with documented pattern.

findings.category: SYSTEM_PROMPT leaks internal ticket reference (DOG-CHAIN-1)
findings.description: Updated instruction reads 'Match answer length...the dogfood F-18 / DOG-CHAIN-1 regression was an over-conservative Be concise prompt'. Embedding internal ticket refs (DOG-CHAIN-1) in the literal SYSTEM_PROMPT sends them verbatim to the LLM on every /ask. Leaks issue-tracking terminology into model context. JSON shape constraints intact — no functional regression. Risk low but adds context noise.
findings.suggestedFix: Move DOG-CHAIN-1 parenthetical to a code comment above SYSTEM_PROMPT constant. Keep the 'Match answer length to question complexity' instruction.

findings.category: rootPath null-byte not blocked on POST /internal/workspaces
findings.description: Internal route schema: rootPath: z.string().min(1).max(1024).optional() — no .refine for null bytes. CLI-facing POST /api/workspaces (createWorkspaceSchema line 112) has the .refine block. Crafted name with null byte reaches graphStore.createWorkspace unsanitized. Risk bounded (authenticated session + server-resolved orgId), but violates 'Validate-then-create' invariant.
findings.suggestedFix: Reuse createWorkspaceSchema from api-workspaces.ts (extract to shared file) or add .refine(s => !s.includes('\0')) inline. Apply to name field too (becomes rootPath via default).

findings.category: CreateWorkspaceModal rootPath sent verbatim — UX guard missing
findings.description: Modal POSTs {name, rootPath}. Web route does best-effort string check but doesn't validate rootPath is plausible absolute path or free of '../' sequences. Authoritative Zod blocks null bytes (after R-08 fix) but not traversal. Risk: graph-data pollution (rootPath stored as Neo4j property), not filesystem access. UX guard prevents confused CLI reconciliation downstream.
findings.suggestedFix: Lightweight regex: rootPath must start with '/' (absolute) or drive letter (Windows). UX-only; authoritative validation stays in Zod.

findings.category: score-coverage-smoke.sh assumes browzer CLI on PATH
findings.description: Script calls browzer explore/search/ask/deps assuming CLI on PATH. e2e-smoke CI runner image may not have it pre-installed. Missing CLI exits with 'command not found' → misleading failure. No `command -v browzer` preflight guard. cli-commands-smoke.sh has same implicit dep.
findings.suggestedFix: Add preflight at top: `command -v browzer >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo 'FAIL: browzer CLI not found'; exit 1; }`. Add explicit install step in e2e-smoke CI setup phase.
